name: dnschef
title: dnschef
description:
DNS proxy for penetration testers
DNSChef is a highly configurable DNS proxy for Penetration
Testers and Malware Analysts. A DNS proxy (aka "Fake DNS")
is a tool used for application network traffic analysis
among other uses. For example, a DNS proxy can be used to
fake requests for "badguy.com" to point to a local machine
for termination or interception instead of a real host
somewhere on the Internet.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dnschef because DNS proxy for penetration testers.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dnschef as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnschef [options] [args]` as documented upstream for dnschef.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dnschef with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dnschef --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
